猜想.摘要: Top1 is neutral aromatic (0.66) and top2 is TICT (0.23). The latest evidence strengthens the current top1: the new planarity/compactness screen reports a highly planar, compact, aromatic-core-dominated hydrocarbon with no hetero atoms and no donor-acceptor partition, which is more consistent with neutral aromatic behavior than with a torsion-enabled CT mechanism. The top2 challenger TICT is weakened but not eliminated, because rotor richness and conformer sensitivity still make it structurally credible, yet the new observable family cuts against the need for strong torsional decoupling. An additional candidate, unknown, also decreases slightly because the evidence remains concentrated in the named neutral aromatic versus TICT competition rather than opening a distinct alternative. TICT and unknown still remain in coverage debt, so the case must stay in portfolio screening and cannot legitimately enter pairwise contraction. The key remaining discriminator is still a direct torsion-coupled CT/localization readout or an explicit blocked-by-capability determination for that route. Because this is round 20 of 20, the best action now is not to pretend the case is pairwise-ready, but to record that portfolio screening ended with unresolved debt and no budget for another bounded direct screen.

This is chemically informative and favors neutral aromatic over TICT, unlike the recent verifier outputs, which were operationally null.
